name: Hawthorn
description: The Hawthorn tree, or Whitethorn (Crataegus monogyna), holds a profound and multifaceted significance in Celtic mythology and folklore. Revered as a sacred tree, it is deeply intertwined with the Otherworld, fairies (the Sidhe), and the realm of dreams. Hawthorn is often found growing in solitary locations, particularly on hills or at crossroads, sites considered liminal spaces where the veil between worlds is thin. It is believed to be a guardian of sacred wells and springs, and its presence is often associated with powerful magic. The tree's delicate white blossoms, followed by vibrant red berries, symbolize both purity and protection. Cutting down a Hawthorn tree was considered extremely unlucky, potentially bringing misfortune or even death to the perpetrator and their family, as it was seen as an act of disrespect to the fairies who dwelled within. Hawthorn branches were used in Beltane celebrations, adorning maypoles and homes to welcome the summer and ensure fertility. Its association with love and marriage is also evident in its use in handfasting ceremonies. The Hawthorn's thorns are seen as a symbol of protection against negative energies and malevolent spirits. It's a potent symbol of hope, renewal, and the cyclical nature of life, death, and rebirth. The scent of the Hawthorn blossom is intoxicating and believed to attract the attention of the fairy folk. The tree serves as a reminder of the importance of respecting the natural world and the unseen forces that inhabit it.
subtitle: Fairy Tree, Protection, Otherworld Gateway

keyMyths.title: Hawthorn and the Fairy Ring
keyMyths.description: Hawthorn trees are often found near fairy rings, marking the entrance to the Otherworld. Entering such a ring without proper respect can lead to being trapped in the fairy realm.
keyMyths.source: Briggs, Katharine Mary. An Encyclopedia of Fairies.

sources.author: Briggs, Katharine Mary
sources.description: Comprehensive guide to fairy lore and beliefs.
sources.text: Comprehensive guide to fairy lore and beliefs.
